summary: Large code-quality refactor that split fighter polymorphic per-entity variables at 0x222C into `Fighter::ev` / `*_EntityVars` and action-state transient variables at 0x2340 into `Fighter::sv` / `*_StateVars`, moving many character-specific layouts out of the monolithic `ft/types.h` into per-character `types.h` and `forward.h` headers. The PR changed 223 files, mostly fighter character sources and headers, replacing older `fp->sa.*` and raw state var fields like `x234C_stateVar4` with named `fp->ev.*` or `fp->sv.*` accesses. It also documented EV/SV terminology in the glossary and made supporting include/build cleanup.
what_changed: Replaced the old `Fighter::sa` special-attributes style storage for many 0x222C fields with a new `Fighter::ev` union described as Entity Variables. Examples in the diff include `fp->sa.clink.x222C` becoming `fp->ev.cl.x222C`, `fp->sa.masterhand.x222C` becoming `fp->ev.mh.x222C`, and Captain Falcon/Ganondorf Raptor Boost flags becoming `fp->ev.ca.during_specials_start` and `fp->ev.ca.during_specials`.

Introduced or renamed per-character state-variable unions using the `ftCharacter_StateVars` pattern, and placed them under `Fighter::sv` at the 0x2340 action-state variable region. Examples include `union ftCaptain_StateVars`, `union ftDonkey_StateVars`, `union ftFox_StateVars`, `union ftGameWatch_StateVars`, `union ftMasterHand_StateVars`, `union ftNess_StateVars`, and others.

Changed many direct raw state fields such as `x234C_stateVar4` into character/action-specific `sv` fields. Donkey Kong SpecialN code, for example, moved copied arm-swing counts from raw state-var fields into `fp->sv.dk.specialn.xC` while persistent arm-swing count stayed in `fp->ev.dk.x222C`.

Added per-character `types.h` and `forward.h` files so character attributes, entity vars, action enums, and state vars no longer all live in the central fighter header. Captain Falcon received a new `ftCaptain/types.h` containing `struct ftCaptain_EntityVars`, `struct ftCaptainAttributes`, and `union ftCaptain_StateVars`.

Reduced `src/melee/ft/types.h` substantially, replacing a large inline set of per-character state layouts with `union FighterEntityVars ev` and `union Fighter_StateVars sv` members that reference per-character types.
Added glossary entries for `EV` as Entity Variables used by `HSD_GObj::user_data` structs such as `Fighter::ev`, and `SV` as State Variables specific to an `ActionState` and used by `Fighter::sv`.

Renamed several identifiers toward project-style names, such as `AS_CAPTAIN_SPECIALS`-style enum entries becoming `ftCaptain_AS_SpecialS`-style entries and `lbl_800E28C8` becoming `ftCaptain_800E28C8`.

Cleaned header include style in many touched files, replacing long `<melee/...>` and old `<Runtime/platform.h>` / `<sysdolphin/baselib/...>` includes with local project includes such as `"ft/types.h"`, `"ft/fighter.h"`, `<platform.h>`, and `<baselib/forward.h>`.
Added Captain Falcon SpecialS header documentation and prototypes in `ftCaptain_SpecialS.h`, separating Side Special declarations from the broad `ftCaptain.h` header.

Made build/tooling side changes: `Makefile` removed `$(*D)` from `INCLUDE_DIRS`, split `$(INCLUDES)` onto its own CFLAGS line, added a `VERBOSE=1` path to pass `-verbose`, and `Cargo.lock` updated Rust dependency versions.
smart_moves: Separated persistent fighter/entity state from transient action-state state instead of continuing to overload `sa` and raw `x2340`-style fields. This makes variable lifetime visible in code: `ev` for non-transient per-fighter data and `sv` for action-state-local scratch data.

Kept the physical offsets explicit in the new structures and documentation, e.g. `ftCaptain_EntityVars` documents 0x222C and 0x2230, while `Fighter_StateVars` is annotated at 0x2340 with a fixed-size backing array. This preserves decomp layout evidence while improving names.
Used unions for per-character polymorphism rather than separate ad hoc fields, matching the underlying memory reuse model of the fighter struct and avoiding layout growth.
Moved character-specific definitions into character-local `types.h`/`forward.h` headers, reducing central header churn and making later per-character decomp work easier to isolate.
Documented abbreviations (`EV`, `SV`, `gr`, `grav`) in `docs/glossary.md`, which helps make the new short member names searchable and consistent across future PRs.

Applied the refactor mechanically across many files while preserving offset names where semantics are still unknown, e.g. `fp->ev.dk.x222C` and unknown nested `sv` fields. This avoids inventing names without evidence while still clarifying lifetime/location.
decomp_lessons: When a reused memory region has two different lifetimes, encode that distinction in the struct model. In this PR, 0x222C data became `ev` Entity Variables, while 0x2340 action-state scratch became `sv` State Variables.

Prefer `ftCharacter_EntityVars` and `ftCharacter_StateVars` types over dumping all per-character fields into `Fighter`. It makes character-specific reverse engineering easier and prevents unrelated fighters from depending on each other’s layouts.
Use unions plus offset comments/filler arrays to model polymorphic game structs accurately without changing ABI or struct size.
Do not force semantic names when only an offset is known. Keeping names like `x222C` under the correct namespace, such as `fp->ev.dk.x222C`, is still an improvement over ambiguous global raw fields.

Move large function prototype blocks and special-move declarations out of broad character headers into move-specific headers when possible. This reduces include coupling and makes matching work less fragile.
Add glossary documentation when introducing short naming conventions such as `ev`, `sv`, `gr`, or `grav`; otherwise future contributors may reinterpret abbreviations inconsistently.
naming_conventions: `Fighter::ev` means Entity Variables: per-fighter polymorphic variables located around 0x222C and stored by `HSD_GObj::user_data` structs.
`Fighter::sv` means State Variables: action-state-specific polymorphic variables located around 0x2340.

Per-character entity/state types use project-style prefixes and underscores, e.g. `struct ftCaptain_EntityVars`, `union ftCaptain_StateVars`, `union ftDonkey_StateVars`, `union ftMasterHand_StateVars`.

Fighter union members use short two-letter character IDs where possible, such as `ca` for Captain, `gn` for Ganondorf, `dk` for Donkey Kong, `fx`/`fc` for Fox/Falco, `mh`/`ch` for Master Hand/Crazy Hand, `mr`/`dr` for Mario/Dr. Mario, and `cl` for Young Link/CLink.
Action enum constants are moved toward `ftCharacter_AS_ActionName`, e.g. `ftCaptain_AS_SpecialN`, `ftCaptain_AS_SpecialAirS`, rather than older all-caps `AS_CAPTAIN_*` names.
Attribute member names are lower_snake_case and move-prefixed, e.g. Captain Falcon attributes like `specialn_stick_range_y_pos`, `specials_grav`, and `specials_terminal_vel`.
Include guards use full path-style uppercase names such as `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTCAPTAIN_TYPES_H` and `MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTCAPTAIN_FTCAPTAIN_SPECIALS_H`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Namespace refactors can improve readability without changing generated code when they only rename struct paths and preserve offsets/types.
For action-state scratch fields, replace raw `x2340`/`x234C` aliases with nested union members matching the action, e.g. `fp->sv.ca.specials.grav` for aerial Raptor Boost gravity.
For persistent object pointers and counters at 0x222C, use `fp->ev.*` rather than `fp->sa.*` so code does not confuse character special attributes with per-entity runtime variables.
Keep unknown fields as offset-style names inside the correct union when semantics are not established; this preserves matching flexibility and avoids misleading reviewers.

Header slimming and forward declarations are useful for large decomp refactors: move full definitions to `types.h`, expose only `forward.h` where possible, and include concrete headers only in `.c` files that need layout access.
The Makefile `VERBOSE=1` addition can help diagnose compiler include/order or CodeWarrior flag issues during large header refactors.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice, so no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue `StateVars` / `EntityVars` pass 2 or finish converting remaining `sa` users.
Search for remaining raw `x2340`, `x2344`, `x2348`, and `x234C_stateVar` fields after PR 764.
Audit all `Fighter::ev` union members that still use `x222C` names and identify candidates for semantic names.
Look for follow-up reviews or regressions related to the Makefile include path removal of `$(*D)`.
Compare later per-character `types.h` patterns against `ftCaptain/types.h` introduced in this PR.
